steps.title: Find the slope of line r
steps.content: Rearrange $4x - 3y = 8$ into slope-intercept form: $$-3y = -4x + 8 \implies y = \frac{4}{3}x - \frac{8}{3}$$ Slope of line $r$ $= \frac{4}{3}$.

steps.title: Test all four choices for the slope of a parallel line
steps.content:
• Choice A — -4: Slope $= -4 eq rac{4}{3}$. Not parallel. ✗
  • Choice B — -3: Slope $= -3 eq rac{4}{3}$. Not parallel. ✗
  • Choice C — $ rac{3}{4}$: Slope $= rac{3}{4} eq rac{4}{3}$. Not parallel (this is the reciprocal, not the same slope). ✗
  • Choice D — $ rac{4}{3}$: Slope $= rac{4}{3}$. Matches slope of line $r$ exactly. Parallel lines have equal slopes. ✓

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: Parallel lines have the same slope. Line $r$ has slope $\frac{4}{3}$, so line $s$ must also have slope $\frac{4}{3}$. That confirms D is correct.
